world_name: Legend of the Five Rings - Rokugan
prompt:
Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: project-bound RPG Japanese feudal fantasy atlas map, first batch pass, landscape orientation
Primary request: Legend of the Five Rings / Rokugan inspired Japanese feudal fantasy map, dual-continent terrain type.
Scene/backdrop: overhead birds-eye atlas view with two samurai-empire landmasses separated by uneven misty seas and narrow straits.
Subject: two asymmetric continents divided by a diagonal S-shaped and C-bending sea corridor, not a simple vertical split. Include island-studded straits, inland lakes, misty gulfs, capes, broken rocky coasts, river deltas, jagged peninsulas, Rokugan-like empire provinces, celestial mountain ranges, corrupt Shadowlands border, castle districts, sacred forest groves, elemental spirit realms beyond the veil, rice plains, cherry blossom valleys, volcanic hills, wetlands, tundra highlands, deep forests, river basins, and coastal ports.
Style/medium: Rokugan map, Japanese feudal empire provinces, elemental rings aesthetic, mon crest-like non-text symbols, ink-and-watercolor atlas, highly detailed illustrated RPG sourcebook cartography.
Composition/framing: wide 16:9 landscape map, both continents fully visible, diagonal/S/C-shaped sea separation, generous sea border, clean top-down readability with painterly terrain.
Lighting/mood: honorable samurai epic, cherry blossom atmosphere, honor and duty, clan politics, court intrigue, elemental spirits, southern corruption and supernatural evil.
Color palette: washi parchment, sumi ink black, muted jade green, cherry blossom pink, imperial red, gold accents, mist blue sea, shadowlands sickly grey-green.
Materials/textures: washi paper, brush ink coastlines, watercolor washes, delicate mon-like symbols without readable characters, mineral pigment terrain.
Constraints: no text labels, no letters, no readable Japanese or Chinese characters, no legend, no compass text, no watermark, no UI, no characters or portraits. Avoid mirrored symmetry and avoid a clean vertical division.
